method_atom: a large-scale systematic measurement-and-cataloging campaign that acquires multi-channel observations (broadband intensity across several filters plus dispersed-signal spectra) of a very large population of individual entities, then improves data quality by cross-calibrating positional and intensity measurements against external reference standards to shrink systematic and statistical errors, coadding repeated measurements over selected regions to reach greater sensitivity, and applying standardized reduction/processing corrections such as flat-fielding, baseline calibration, and robust handling of anomalous cases.
object_atom: the sky and its astronomical sources — 357 million imaged objects with five-band photometry plus 1.6 million spectra of galaxies, quasars, and stars — covered by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey imaging and spectroscopic footprint.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the bulk multi-channel survey/cataloging-with-cross-calibration-and-coaddition methodology; the domain-specific part is the celestial sky and its stars, galaxies, and quasars, which as a data-release descriptor is closely fused with but still separable from the method.
